title: Sucrose Preference Test
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"557nb","text":"The sucrose preference test (SPT) is used to assess anhedonia, a core symptom of depression and motivational dysfunction, which are common non-motor symptoms of PD.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"822la","text":"If you are using the Petco bottles be careful with leaks. We puff air out of the bottle to avoid liquid loss because of air dilation. For the same reason avoid temperature fluctuations in the room where you are performing the test.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Group-housed animals should first be habituated for 24 hours to consume fluids from tube bottlesand to adjust to the 2% sucrose solution
After habituation, group-housed mice should br individually caged, and present two bottles to them for 24 hours: one with tap water and one with a 2% sucrose solution.
Take initial weight of water or 2% sucrose solution bottles. Consumption of either solution is measured by weighing bottles every 24 hr for 48 hours total.
The sucrose preference should be measured over 48 hours.
Weigh animals after last day of testing
Calculate sucrose preference: Preference ml/g/day = [sucrose solution intake (ml)/ sucrose solution intake (ml)+water solution intake (mL)*100].
creator: Jacquelyn Haytayan
